Documentation.short_help: Subtract a (transformable) OBB from a closed surface; transformed OBB on port 1.
Documentation.long_help: Computes a PCA-style oriented bounding box from the selected region, applies Position / Rotation / Scale consistent with the ParaView Interactive Box (unit reference bounds and scale as world edge lengths along the box axes), then runs CGAL boolean difference (input minus box). Port 0 is the result; port 1 is the transformed OBB mesh. The custom box widget reads OBB field arrays from the client-side output when available and pushes Position / Rotation / Scale onto the filter proxy so the 3D widget is initialized to the fitted OBB (not only the mesh AABB). Input must be a closed triangulated surface suitable for vtkCGALBooleanOperation.

InputProperty.name: Input
InputProperty.Documentation: Closed triangulated surface (first operand of the difference).

InputProperty.name: Selection
InputProperty.label: Selection
InputProperty.Documentation: Region used to collect points for the OBB. Use Copy Active Selection in ParaView when Use Selection Input is on.

IntVectorProperty.name: UseSelectionInput
IntVectorProperty.label: Use Selection Input (Copy Active Selection)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When on (default), the Selection port is read when connected (use Copy Active Selection to push the interactive selection into this filter). When off, the Selection port is ignored and only Selection Cell Array Name defines the region.

IntVectorProperty.name: CopyInputPointsForOBB
IntVectorProperty.label: Copy Input Points For OBB
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Passed to SHYX Minimum OBB: when on, selected points are copied before OBB computation.

IntVectorProperty.name: UpdateAttributes
IntVectorProperty.label: Interpolate attributes
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: If ON, point/cell data are interpolated onto the boolean result (CGAL pipeline).

StringVectorProperty.name: SelectionCellArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Selection Cell Array Name
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Optional fallback when no selection points were obtained from port 1: name of a cell data array on Input; non-zero (integral) or > 0.5 (float) marks selected cells. All vertices of those cells contribute to the OBB point set.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Position
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Translate
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: World translation applied after RotateZ, RotateX, RotateY and Scale about the OBB center from field data (same convention as ParaView Interactive Box).

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Rotation
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Rotate (Degrees)
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Degrees about the OBB center: RotateZ (Z), then RotateX (X), then RotateY (Y), matching ParaView Interactive Box (pqBoxPropertyWidget).

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Scale
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Scale
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: World edge lengths of the box for a 0..1 reference range on each axis (same as Interactive Box). The filter maps these onto the raw OBB mesh using the fitted half-lengths from vtkSHYXMinimumOBBFilter. The XML default (1,1,1) with zero Position/Rotation is treated as not yet initialized so the first pipeline update matches the raw OBB mesh; the custom box widget then sets Scale to 2*h. Non-positive components are clamped for a stable boolean operation.

IntVectorProperty.name: UseReferenceBounds
IntVectorProperty.label: Use Reference Bounds
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Internal: Interactive Box uses explicit reference bounds (OBB axis-aligned bounds).

DoubleVectorProperty.name: ReferenceBounds
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Reference Bounds
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Internal: unit box (0..1) when fitted OBB field data exists; otherwise axis-aligned bounds of the OBB mesh for placement.
